key_insight: vitriflow turns melt–quench amorphous-structure generation into an explicit, calibrated decision chain that links simulation settings, screening, and convergence to the material being modeled.
engineering_relevance: It provides a reproducible way to build and filter amorphous ensembles with protocol choices justified by the target material rather than by fixed defaults.
method: Calibrate numerical stability and melt/quench protocol parameters in a material-specific descriptor space.
Apply user-defined artefact screening to remove defective or recrystallised structures.
Check statistical convergence of the analysis ensemble after generation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on three materials—a-SiO2, a-Si3N4, and a-Sm2O3—showing defect separation, DFT-refinement response, and recrystallisation removal.
watch_item: Performance depends on the chosen descriptors and screening rules, so improper calibration could bias which amorphous structures are kept.
paper_title: Vitriflow: calibrated amorphous structure ensembles from melt-quench simulation
